steps: Vote to dissolve per operating agreement or majority vote of members
Wind up business affairs, settle debts, and distribute remaining assets
File Certificate of Dissolution online through Secretary of State website ($50 fee)
File final Mississippi state tax returns
Close all tax accounts with the Mississippi Department of Revenue
Cancel any DBAs, licenses, and permits
llc.notes: Filing is done online through the Secretary of State's Business Services portal. Create a corporate filing account or log in with an existing account, then select 'File Articles of Dissolution or Certificate of Withdrawal.'

steps: Board of directors adopts resolution recommending dissolution
Shareholders approve dissolution by majority vote
Wind up business affairs, notify creditors, and settle debts
File Articles of Dissolution online through Secretary of State website ($25 fee)
File final Mississippi state tax returns
Cancel any DBAs, licenses, and permits
corporation.notes: Corporation dissolution filing fee is $25, lower than the LLC dissolution fee of $50. File online through the Secretary of State's Business Services portal.

tax.notes: File final Mississippi income tax return and mark as final. Close all tax accounts with the Department of Revenue. Notify the DOR of dissolution to prevent future tax obligations.

requirements.employeeNotification: Follow federal WARN Act if 100+ employees
requirements.creditorNotification: Must notify known creditors in writing; unknown creditors may be notified by publication
requirements.registeredAgentNotes: Registered agent must be maintained until dissolution is complete
requirements.windingUpPeriod: Reasonable time to wind up affairs after dissolution is filed
requirements.boardOrMemberApproval: LLCs require majority vote of members. Corporations require board resolution and majority shareholder approval.

includes: Every required form with direct download links
